text: Uncertainty estimation for well-calibrated predictive models is a crucial aspect in most applications of artificial intelligence. In this paper, we introduce Bayesian Binning into Quantiles (BBQ) – a non-parametric post-processing method to calibrate probabilistic models. BBQ overcomes many of the existing challenges associated with other calibration methods, and as a post-processing method for the output of a base binary classifier, it can be seamlessly applied to almost all machine learning models. We present extensive experimental comparisons with other calibration methods on simulated and several benchmark real-world datasets. Our results show that the BBQ model is computationally efficient, and often provides the most accurate calibration performance.
